phase3_findings.description: path-portability-test.sh header claimed it stubs wslpath/cygpath/uname; the body correctly omits wslpath/cygpath (only uname/MSYSTEM stubbed) — mirror-fact drift.
unchecked mkdir/cp -R in _seed_repo; a failed copy is caught downstream but attributed to a confusing later step.
AC7 join depth (../../scripts) is hardcoded in the driver, not sourced from the live SKILL anchor string; a production anchor-depth regression would not be caught.
AC7 consumer layout is hand-crafted, not coupled to install.sh's actual vendored path; an installer path change would not be caught here.
AC7 executes via bash <path>, a shape the real cloud runner deny-floors; this is a resolution test not a permission test (shapes covered separately by extract-command-shapes.py).

fix_decisions.claim_text: AC7 join depth hardcoded, not sourced from the live SKILL anchor
fix_decisions.evidence: AC7 requires executing the cloud form against the two layouts with spaces + shallow-detached represented; coupling the fixture depth to the live SKILL anchor string is a broader robustness enhancement beyond the AC's wording — surfaced for follow-up.

parking_evidence.basis: the deferred check exceeds AC7's stated scope (resolution across layouts/properties), not a defect in what the AC requires
parking_evidence.failing_input: a future SKILL anchor drifting to a different ../ depth

fix_decisions.claim_text: consumer layout not coupled to install.sh vendored path
fix_decisions.evidence: AC7 requires the consumer layout be represented (it is); coupling it to install.sh's actual copy destination is a separate installer-contract test — surfaced for follow-up.

parking_evidence.basis: the AC requires representation of the consumer layout, not an install.sh coupling assertion
parking_evidence.failing_input: install.sh changing its vendored copy destination

fix_decisions.claim_text: executes via bash <path> which the cloud runner deny-floors
fix_decisions.evidence: This is a path-resolution integration test, not a permission-shape test; cloud command-shape denial is covered separately by extract-command-shapes.py. No AC7 coverage gap.

parking_evidence.basis: resolution test vs permission test are distinct concerns; the latter is covered by extract-command-shapes.py
parking_evidence.failing_input: n/a — not a coverage gap against AC7 wording
